data.title: Seagate Technology Holdings PLC reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Seagate Technology Holdings PLC reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $4.10​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.90. The mean expectation of twenty analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $3.51 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $3.40 to $3.70 per share.

  • Revenue rose 44.1% to $3.11 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $2.96 billion.

  • Seagate Technology Holdings PLC's reported EPS for the quarter was $3.27​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $748 million.

  • Seagate Technology Holdings PLC shares had risen by 52.1% this quarter and gained 116.4% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 8.8% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, one analyst negatively revised an earnings estimate

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 22 "strong buy" or "buy," 3 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the computer hardware peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Seagate Technology Holdings PLC is $530.00, about 12.4% below its last closing price of $595.86

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data April 28 at 09:38 p.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Ingersoll Rand Logs Higher 1Q Profit, Revenue
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Ingersoll Rand posted higher profit in the fiscal first quarter as revenue in both its segments grew.

The life sciences and industrial solutions company on Tuesday reported a profit of $192.1 million, or 49 cents a share, compared with a profit of $186.5 million, or 46 cents a share, a year earlier.

On an adjusted basis, the company logged earnings of 77 cents a share, ahead of analysts' forecast of 74 cents a share, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose to $1.85 billion from $1.72 a year earlier. Analysts were expecting $1.83 billion, according to FactSet.

Industrial technologies and services segment revenue grew 7% to $1.44 billion. Precision and science technologies revenue was up 10% to $402.7 million.

The company said it is maintaining its full-year guidance. It expects revenue growth of 2.5% to 4.5%, adjusted earnings per share of $3.45 to $3.57. Analysts are expecting adjusted per-share earnings of $3.51.

Ingersoll Rand said earlier this month it was acquiring Fox s.r.l., a manufacturer of hydropneumatic accumulators and pulsation dampeners, the company said. The transaction is expected to close on April 30, the company said.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Improved Canadian Debt, Deficit Metrics Deserve Recognition — Market Talk
data.description: The upwardly revised levels of Canadian nominal GDP will reduce the size of projected budget deficits as a share of the economy, says Jimmy Jean, chief economist at Desjardins Group. He adds the projected path for the federal debt-to-GDP ratio is also meaningfully lower. His analysis, following the release of Canada's economic and fiscal update, suggests the improvement in Canadian public finances reflects a better-than-expected economic performance, as opposed to newfound fiscal discipline from PM Mark Carney's Liberal government. He says risks to the economic outlook are tilted to the downside, amid higher energy prices and trade-policy uncertainty. Still, the update showed improved deficit and debt metrics, and that deserves recognition, Jean concludes. (Paul.Vieira@wsj.com, @paulvieira)

data.title: Seagate forecasts upbeat quarter as AI boom powers strong data-storage demand
data.description:
Seagate Technology forecast fourth-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations on Tuesday, betting on strong demand for its data-storage hardware as enterprises ramp up adoption of artificial intelligence.

Shares of the company surged around 16% in extended trading, fueling a rise in rivals' stock. Western Digital jumped 11% and SanDisk rose 4%. Memory chipmaker Micron Technology gained about 3.5%.

Enterprises rushing to incorporate AI to speed up processes and cut costs have also invested in storage to handle the vast amount of data required to create and run the latest models.

The surge in memory chip prices has also boosted investor enthusiasm in the storage market, with Seagate's shares doubling in value so far this year after more than tripling in 2025.

Seagate forecast fourth-quarter revenue of $3.45 billion, plus or minus $100 million, compared with estimates of $3.16 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company projected quarterly adjusted earnings per share of $5, plus or minus 20 cents, while analysts were expecting $3.97.

Seagate reported third-quarter revenue of $3.11 billion, beating estimates of $2.96 billion.

It reported earnings per share of $3.27, compared with $1.57 per share a year earlier.

data.title: Authentic Brands Group CEO says retailer could top $50 billion in 2026 revenue through deals
data.description:
By Arriana McLymore

Guess-owner Authentic Brands Group plans to again attempt to go public through an IPO "soon," Chief Executive Jamie Salter said at the Reuters Momentum AI event in New York on Tuesday.

Salter, who is also the founder and chairman of the group, said the company is ahead on its five-year goal of $100 billion in sales.

"If we pull off the deals that we're currently working on, we'll be over $50 billion (in sales) by the end of this year," Salter said in an interview. "We told our board it would take us five years, but I think we will reach it much quicker than anticipated."

  • The group, which owns Dockers and Brooks Brothers, is expected to have about $38 billion in sales this year if there are no M&A transactions.

  • Salter said that AI is helping the company's business development team execute more deals more quickly than in previous years.

  • Authentic Brands Group is now focusing its M&A strategy to include kids entertainment brands and hospitality.

  • The company previously filed to go public twice. "Both times, we got bought out for way more than we were going public for," Salter said.

  • Salter did not provide details on exactly when the company plans to file an initial public offering with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. He said he plans lead the company in a role other than CEO once that happens.

data.title: Rush Enterprises Q1 revenue falls on weak new vehicle sales
data.description:
Overview

  • US commercial vehicle dealer's Q1 revenue fell and missed analyst expectations

  • Net income and EPS rose yr/yr despite lower sales, aided by aftermarket and leasing growth

  • Company to acquire six dealerships in Louisiana and Mississippi to expand network

Outlook

  • Company expects commercial vehicle sales to improve gradually beginning in the second quarter

  • Aftermarket demand expected to gradually improve through remainder of 2026 as fleet utilization rises

  • Leasing and rental business projected to remain stable and strengthen as market conditions improve

Result Drivers

  • WEAK NEW VEHICLE SALES - Co said revenue decline was driven by historically low new commercial vehicle sales due to prolonged freight recession, excess capacity and economic uncertainty

  • AFTERMARKET GROWTH - Modest growth in aftermarket parts, service and collision center revenues supported results despite industry softness

  • LEASING AND RENTAL - Leasing and rental revenue rose, driven by continued strength in full-service leasing operations

data.title: Volato to Combine with M2i Global in Reverse Acquisition; Pro forma assets $20.9M, $2.8M goodwill
data.description:
Volato announced a proposed reverse acquisition of M2i Global and filed unaudited pro forma combined financials showing combined pro forma assets of $20.9 million and preliminary goodwill of $2.8 million.

Key Highlights:

  • Volato will merge Merger Sub into M2i Global; M2i Global to survive as a wholly‑owned subsidiary pending shareholder approval.
  • Special meeting of Volato shareholders set for May 7, 2026; proxy/prospectus filed and mailed to record holders as of April 17, 2026.
  • Pro forma combined assets of $20.9M as of Dec 31, 2025 and pro forma shareholders’ equity of $7.1M (unaudited).
  • Estimated preliminary purchase price of $8.9M; Volato shareholders expected to own ~15% of the combined company.
  • Transaction treated as a reverse acquisition with preliminary goodwill of $2.8M and identifiable intangibles of $1.2M.

Original SEC Filing: Volato Group, Inc. [ SOAR ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

data.title: Sun Country Airlines Holdings supplements proxy with forecasts, faces litigation ahead of May 8 vote
data.description:
Sun Country Airlines Holdings supplemented its joint proxy/prospectus with Allegiant, disclosing updated management forecasts and noting two lawsuits challenging the merger.

Key Highlights:

  • Allegiant's Form S-4 declared effective; joint proxy/prospectus mailed and special stockholder meetings set for May 8, 2026.
  • Two lawsuits filed in New York County Supreme Court and additional demand letters allege disclosure deficiencies; company denies merits.
  • Supplemental disclosures add detailed Allegiant and Sun Country management forecasts, including projected revenues, adjusted EBITDAR and free cash flow.
  • Goldman Sachs and Barclays analyses included illustrative DCF, premia and implied per-share valuation ranges for standalone and pro forma scenarios.

Original SEC Filing: Sun Country Airlines Holdings, Inc. [ SNCY ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

  • LOI non-binding except for confidentiality, expenses, exclusivity and other specified provisions; deal not assured.

data.title: Pyrophyte Acquisition Adds $1.2M Extension Note, Extends Convertible Working Capital Facility to 2027
data.description:
Pyrophyte Acquisition entered two financing arrangements with its Sponsor to support an extension of its business combination timeline and fund ongoing expenses. The company issued an interest-free promissory note of up to $1.2 million to cover monthly extension contributions, repayable upon the earlier of a business combination or liquidation. It also amended and restated its unsecured working capital convertible promissory note, moving maturity to the earlier of April 29, 2027 or deal closing, with an option to convert up to $1.5 million into warrants at $1.00 per warrant, exercisable at $11.50 per share. These steps aim to preserve liquidity and flexibility while Pyrophyte pursues a merger target.

Agreement 1: Pyrophyte Acquisition Issues Up to $1.2 Million Extension Note to Sponsor to Fund Trust Contributions

  • Agreement type: Unsecured interest-free promissory note for monthly extension contributions
  • Counterparty: Pyrophyte Acquisition (Sponsor)
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 28 2026 / Apr 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until initial business combination or liquidation
  • Reason: Provide liquidity for SPAC extension and trust contributions

Agreement 2: Pyrophyte Acquisition Extends Working Capital Note; Convertible Into Up to 1.5 Million Warrants

  • Agreement type: Amended and restated unsecured working capital convertible promissory note
  • Counterparty: Pyrophyte Acquisition (Sponsor)
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 28 2026 / Apr 28 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until Apr 29 2027 or earlier upon business combination
  • Reason: Extend runway and finance working capital pending business combination

Original SEC Filing: Pyrophyte Acquisition Corp. [ PHYT ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: P3 Health Partners Trades $252.5M Debt for Preferred, Adds Up to $70M CPF Financing
data.description:
P3 Health Partners executed a series of financing transactions with Chicago Pacific Founders affiliates to strengthen its equity base and improve Nasdaq compliance. The company exchanged approximately $252.5 million of outstanding notes into new non-convertible cumulative preferred stock across Series A (13.5%), Series B (17.5%), and Series C (19.5%), redeemable at $100 per share. It also launched up to $70 million of Series D preferred units with seven-year warrants, closing an initial $10 million tranche. Related agreements provide registration rights for warrant shares and extend CPF's standstill to January 1, 2027 while permitting an additional independent board designee, subject to ownership thresholds.

Agreement 1: P3 Health Partners Swaps $252.5M Debt for New Cumulative Preferred to Regain Nasdaq Compliance

  • Agreement type: Debt Exchange Agreement for issuance of non-convertible cumulative preferred stock
  • Counterparty: Chicago Pacific Founders affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Regain Nasdaq equity compliance and deleverage balance sheet

Agreement 2: P3 Health Partners Launches Up to $70M Series D Preferred and Warrants With CPF; $10M Closed

  • Agreement type: Securities purchase agreement for Series D preferred units with warrants
  • Counterparty: Chicago Pacific Founders affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Strengthen liquidity and equity base with strategic sponsor support

Agreement 3: P3 Health Partners Grants Registration Rights for Warrant Shares Issued to CPF Affiliates

  • Agreement type: Registration rights agreement for warrant shares
  • Counterparty: Chicago Pacific Founders affiliates
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Facilitate potential resale of warrant shares

Agreement 4: P3 Health Partners, CPF Extend Standstill and Add Board Designee Under Amended Letter

  • Agreement type: Third amended and restated governance letter with standstill and board designation
  • Counterparty: Chicago Pacific Founders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 27 2026 / Apr 27 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Through Jan 01 2027
  • Reason: Align governance and cap ownership while securing sponsor support

Original SEC Filing: P3 Health Partners Inc. [ PIII ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Lunai Bioworks postpones special meeting to May 8 to allow broker discretionary voting
data.description:
Lunai Bioworks postponed its Special Meeting of Stockholders to May 8, 2026 to provide more time for voting and to help obtain a quorum.

Key Highlights:

  • Special Meeting moved from May 4 to May 8, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. ET; virtual-only via www.virtualshareholdermeeting.com/LNAI2026SM
  • Postponement intended to give stockholders extra time to vote and assist the company in obtaining a quorum
  • Delay follows Broadridge distribution on April 22, which may have prevented broker discretionary voting timing under NYSE Rule 452
  • Company expects postponement will permit brokers to exercise discretionary voting on matters likely classified as routine, including the reverse stock split proposal
  • Record date remains close of business April 10, 2026; previously submitted proxies will be voted as postponed unless revoked

Original SEC Filing: Lunai Bioworks Inc. [ LNAI ] - 8-K - Apr. 28, 2026

data.title: Allegiant to combine with Sun Country; files S-4, discloses forecasts and litigation
data.description:
Allegiant announced supplemental disclosures to its joint proxy/prospectus for the proposed merger with Sun Country and disclosed management forecasts and related litigation.

Key Highlights:

  • Allegiant filed an S-4; registration declared effective March 31, 2026 and joint proxy/prospectus mailed to stockholders.
  • Special meetings of Allegiant and Sun Country stockholders set for May 8, 2026 to vote on the mergers.
  • Two lawsuits and several demand letters allege disclosure deficiencies; Allegiant and Sun Country deny claims but issued supplemental disclosures to moot challenges.
  • Included management forecasts: Allegiant projects 2026 net income $137M, operating revenue $2,664M and 2026 adjusted EBITDAR $550M; Sun Country projects 2026 operating revenue $1,178M and adjusted EBITDAR $209M.

data.title: Equity Residential forecasts second-quarter FFO below estimates
data.description:
Equity Residential forecast second-quarter funds from operations below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday as demand for rental apartments remained weak and supply stayed high.

The Chicago, Illinois-based company's results come as U.S. apartment landlords operate in a tougher market, with a wave of new buildings competing for tenants and slowing rent growth, while rising costs and higher interest rates weigh on the sector.

  • Shares of the company were down 1.7% after the bell.

  • The real estate investment trust expects second-quarter adjusted FFO in the range of 98 cents to $1.02 per share, the midpoint of which is below analysts' estimates of $1.02, according to data compiled by LSEG.

  • Equity Residential's quarterly profit fell to 24 cents per share compared with 67 cents per share a year earlier.

  • Its first-quarter adjusted FFO came in at 99 cents, compared with analysts' estimates of 98 cents.

  • Quarterly revenue rose to $779.8 million compared with $760.8 million a year earlier.

data.title: F5 raises annual revenue growth forecast on strong security spending
data.description:
Cybersecurity firm F5 raised its annual revenue growth forecast on Tuesday, betting on strong demand for its application and cloud security products as enterprises shore up digital protection efforts.

With the rapid advancement of artificial intelligence, companies are looking to modernize and upgrade their digital infrastructure, a trend benefiting companies such as F5 that provide web security and protection from bad actors.

  • Shares of the company rose around 5% in extended trading.

  • The company raised its annual revenue growth forecast to between 7% and 8%, compared with its earlier expectation of between 5% and 6%.

  • It forecast third-quarter revenue of between $820 million and $840 million, compared with estimates of $818.8 million, according to data compiled by LSEG.

  • F5 reported second-quarter revenue of $812 million, beating estimates of $781 million.

  • The company reported profit per share of $2.58, compared with a profit of $2.48 per share, a year ago.

data.title: Distribution Solutions Group Announces Timing for First Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Results
data.description:
FORT WORTH, Texas--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 28, 2026--

Distribution Solutions Group, Inc. ("DSG" or the "Company"), a premier, multi-platform distribution company, today announced that it will release its financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, on Thursday, April 30, 2026, pre-market.

On March 14, 2026, the Company received an unsolicited preliminary, non-binding proposal from LKCM Headwater Investments, LLC, together with its affiliates and related parties which includes LKCM (the "LKCM Group"), to acquire all of the outstanding shares of the Company's common stock not currently owned by LKCM Group for $29.50 per share in cash (the "Take-Private Proposal"). As of the date of such filing, LKCM Group, together with its affiliates, beneficially owned approximately 78.7% of the Company's outstanding shares of common stock. Given the Take-Private Proposal, the Company will not host a conference call to discuss first quarter 2026 results.

The earnings release and accompanying financial information will be available on the Company's investor relations website at Investor Relations | Distribution Solutions Group.

Consistent with its practice, the Company does not comment on market rumors or speculation, including those regarding potential strategic alternatives.

About Distribution Solutions Group, Inc.

Distribution Solutions Group ("DSG") is a leading multi-platform specialty distribution company providing high-touch, value-added distribution solutions to the Maintenance, Repair & Operations (MRO), Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) and industrial technologies markets. DSG was formed through the strategic combination of Lawson Products, a leader in MRO distribution of C-parts, Gexpro Services, a leading global supply chain services provider to manufacturing customers, and TestEquity, a leader in electronic test & measurement solutions.

Through its collective businesses, DSG is dedicated to helping customers reduce total costs of operation by improving productivity and efficiency with the right products, expert technical support and fast, reliable delivery to be a one-stop solution provider. DSG serves approximately 200,000 customers across diverse end markets, supported by approximately 4,400 dedicated employees and strong vendor partnerships. DSG ships from strategically located distribution and service centers to customers in North America, Europe, Asia, South America and the Middle East.

For more information on Distribution Solutions Group, please visit www.distributionsolutionsgroup.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260428298964/en/
